key_learnings: Mapped the relationship between gut microbiome composition and macronutrient metabolism; identified how probiotic-rich foods promote beneficial bacterial growth; learned that dietary fiber acts as a prebiotic supporting microbiome diversity.
research_findings: Specific gut bacteria strains (e.g., Lactobacillus and Bifidobacterium) significantly influence nutrient absorption and immune response; low microbiome diversity is correlated with higher rates of obesity and metabolic syndrome; fermented foods measurably increase microbial richness within 6 weeks of consistent consumption.
current_focus_areas: Analyzing the impact of antibiotic use on long-term microbiome recovery; mapping individual microbiome profiles to personalized dietary recommendations; studying the gut-brain axis and its implications for mood and food cravings.
